extra.reasoning:
The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. The transition [t_resume_glitch] on send_attempt with guard (opted_out and ticks >= 2) sets sent := true while opted_out is true, directly contradicting the invariant. The guard is reachable.

Trace of witness sequence (initial: opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0):
1. optout -> [t_optout] (guard true): opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0.
2. tick -> [t_tick]: ticks=1, sent=false; opted_out remains true (no transition clears it).
3. tick -> [t_tick]: ticks=2, sent=false; opted_out still true.
4. send_attempt: enabled transitions are [t_send_blocked] (guard opted_out=true) and [t_resume_glitch] (guard opted_out and ticks>=2 = true). Because the property must hold on EVERY reachable execution, the existence of the enabled [t_resume_glitch] transition (the later, more-specific rule) means the system can fire it, yielding state opted_out=true, sent=true, ticks=2.

This reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true, violating opted_out implies not sent. The counterexample uses 4 events, within the 6-event bound. The transition is aptly named "resume_glitch": it re-enables sending after opt-out once two ticks elapse, defeating the opt-out guarantee.
